title: Merck — HIV — Latest Briefing
html:
What Changed in HIV (Merck) — Q1 2026

Generated: 2026-04-24 Quarter covered: January – March 2026 (with April 2026 FDA approval update) Data sources: CROI 2026 (Feb 2026), FDA IDVYNSO approval (Apr 21, 2026), Merck press releases, ZoomRx 2026 SFE planning document, analyst projections Reading time: ~15 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. FDA approved IDVYNSO (doravirine/islatravir) on April 21, 2026 — 7 days ahead of PDUFA. First non-INSTI, tenofovir-free, once-daily, complete 2-drug regimen. Available in pharmacies from May 11, 2026. Promotions begin May 1. This is the defining moment for Merck's HIV franchise.
  2. CROI 2026 late-breaking data from three Phase 3 trials — DOR/ISL demonstrated non-inferior efficacy to Biktarvy at Week 48 in treatment-naive adults AND maintained virologic suppression at Week 96 in switch patients. The treatment-naive data is the game-changer — it opens the door to a label expansion that would make IDVYNSO competitive for ALL HIV patients, not just switch.
  3. Merck will promote ONLY IDVYNSO — confirmed in 2026 SFE planning document. No more Pifeltro promotions. Full sales force pivot to a single product. This simplifies the rep's job but raises the stakes — every call must land the IDVYNSO story.
  4. ZoomRx DOR/ISL SFE study locked: 2 waves in 2H 2026, N=90/wave, tracking DOR/ISL vs Dovato vs Biktarvy. First topline by late July, 4-6 weeks post-launch. This is the first external measurement of IDVYNSO commercial execution.
  5. Analysts project IDVYNSO at ~$1.8B by 2033 (7MM) — modest vs Biktarvy's $13B+, but transformative for Merck's HIV franchise. Merck frames the total HIV opportunity at $15B by mid-2030s including islatravir pipeline combinations.

2. Data Events That Matter

FDA Approves IDVYNSO — Merck Enters the Big Leagues in HIV

  • What happened: On April 21, 2026, the FDA approved IDVYNSO (doravirine 100mg / islatravir 0.25mg) for the treatment of HIV-1 infection in virologically suppressed adults (RNA <50 copies/mL) switching from a stable ART regimen. No history of virologic treatment failure. No known doravirine resistance substitutions. This is NDA 216964.
  • What it means: Merck now has a product with genuine competitive differentiation:
    • First non-INSTI complete 2-drug regimen — for INSTI-averse/intolerant patients
    • First tenofovir-free complete 2-drug regimen — eliminates renal/bone concerns
    • First-in-class NRTTI — islatravir represents a novel mechanism
    • Head-to-head Phase 3 data vs Biktarvy — non-inferior efficacy at Week 48
    • Favorable weight and lipid profile — addresses the INSTI weight concern
  • What Vinoth should say: "This is the first time Merck has had an HIV product that can compete on clinical data, not just niche positioning. The question now is purely commercial: can your field force translate this data into prescription share against Gilead's dominant infrastructure? Our SFE tracking starts in July — we'll have your first answer by August."

CROI 2026: Three Phase 3 Trials — The Full Data Package

  • What happened: Merck presented late-breaking data from MK-8591A-051 (treatment-naive), MK-8591A-052 (switch from BIC/FTC/TAF), and MK-8591A-053 (switch from other oral ART) at CROI 2026 in February.

MK-8591A-051 (Treatment-Naive):

  • DOR/ISL demonstrated non-inferior efficacy to BIC/FTC/TAF at Week 48 in >500 treatment-naive adults
  • High rates of virologic suppression across subgroups including high baseline viral loads and low CD4 counts
  • Safety and tolerability comparable to BIC/FTC/TAF
  • This is the FIRST non-INSTI 2-drug regimen to achieve non-inferiority vs Biktarvy in treatment-naive patients

MK-8591A-052/053 (Switch):

  • DOR/ISL maintained virologic suppression at Week 96 in adults who switched from BIC/FTC/TAF or other oral ART

  • Safety comparable to staying on prior regimen

  • What it means: The treatment-naive data changes the strategic calculus. The current IDVYNSO label is switch-only, but the Week 48 non-inferiority data in treatment-naive patients supports a supplemental NDA. If Merck files and gets the treatment-naive indication, IDVYNSO's addressable market expands from "suppressed patients considering a switch" to "ALL adults initiating or maintaining HIV treatment."

  • What Vinoth should say: "The CROI treatment-naive data is the single most important clinical result for Merck HIV in a decade. Non-inferior to Biktarvy in new patients — not just switch. When the label expands, IDVYNSO goes from a switch option to a first-line contender. That changes the size of the tracking study, the competitor set, and the commercial investment needed."

2026 ZoomRx DOR/ISL SFE Study Design — Measurement Blueprint

  • What happened: The study design is finalized: 2 waves in 2H 2026, N=90 unique HCPs per wave (mix of IDs and PCPs, MDs and NP/PAs), tracking DOR/ISL vs Dovato vs Biktarvy. 150 TP1 interactions per wave (50 each brand), 150 NPP responses. Immediate recall (TP1) within days of interaction, delayed recall (TP2) 2 weeks later. Message effectiveness and stickiness tracked.
  • What it means: This is the measurement architecture for IDVYNSO's commercial launch. The study tracks everything that matters: reach, SOV, message recall, message effectiveness, rep performance, interaction outcomes, prescribing intent, product perceptions. Wave 1 topline by week of July 27, full report by week of September 7.
  • Key design considerations:
    • Assume reps promote ONLY DOR/ISL (no Pifeltro)
    • 4-6 week buffer post-launch before fielding begins
    • HCPs from Merck's ~6K target list
    • Lookback window: 20 days (may decrease if field force expands or call frequency increases)

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Note: Merck Q1 2026 earnings are scheduled for April 30, 2026 — not yet reported. HIV-specific revenue is bundled in Other Pharmaceuticals. Competitor data below reflects reported Q1 2026 earnings where available. IDVYNSO was approved April 21, 2026 — no commercial revenue this quarter.

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Gilead Biktarvy ~$3.5B +6% Continued dominance; guided HIV franchise to $20B+ FY2026
Gilead Sunlenca (lenacapavir) ~$120M N/A (ramp) Accelerating; PURPOSE 2 data expected; positioned as most significant HIV advance since Biktarvy
GSK/ViiV Cabenuva ~$450M +20% Long-acting treatment leadership established; infrastructure moat
GSK/ViiV Dovato ~$450M Stable Flat; ViiV de-emphasizing oral investment
GSK/ViiV Apretude ~$130M Growing LA PrEP growing; acknowledged Sunlenca competitive threat
IDVYNSO $0 (approved Apr 21) N/A Approved; promotions begin May 1; pharmacy availability May 11

Gilead Sciences — Biktarvy ($3.5B, +6%) + Sunlenca ($120M)

Gilead Q1 2026 call (April 2026):

  1. Biktarvy guided to $14.5B+ FY2026, maintaining 70%+ new-start share: Gilead's Q1 commentary reinforced that Biktarvy remains the unchallenged standard of care. Despite IDVYNSO's NDA acceptance (known since Q4 2025), Gilead reported no erosion in new-start share. Management stated that "Biktarvy's 7-year efficacy and safety track record" is the primary physician loyalty driver.

  2. Lenacapavir accelerating to ~$120M quarterly with PURPOSE 2 data expected: Sunlenca's growth trajectory is steepening — from $50M (Q3) to $80M (Q4) to $120M (Q1). Gilead described lenacapavir as "the most significant advance in HIV since Biktarvy" and positioned it as a platform across treatment, prevention, and cure research. PURPOSE 2 (PrEP in cis men, trans men/women) data is expected to complement PURPOSE 1's 100% efficacy in cis women.

  3. Gilead guided total HIV franchise to $20B+ for FY2026: This represents the single largest HIV franchise in pharmaceutical history. The commercial infrastructure — field force, MSL network, payer relationships, formulary position — is unmatched. IDVYNSO launches into a market where Gilead's presence is near-ubiquitous.

  4. Gilead's IDVYNSO counter-strategy is forming: Analyst Q&A revealed Gilead's anticipated response — emphasize Biktarvy's proven track record over IDVYNSO's novelty, question the clinical significance of non-INSTI differentiation in most patients, and highlight the unknown long-term islatravir profile vs. 7 years of Biktarvy real-world data.

Implication for Merck: IDVYNSO launches on May 1 into the most competitive HIV market in history. Gilead's counter-detailing strategy is already visible: "why risk a new product when Biktarvy works?" Merck's response must be patient-specific, not population-level — target the INSTI-intolerant, weight-concerned, renal-impaired, and "want something different" patients. ZoomRx's SFE Wave 1 (July) must capture whether Merck reps are leading with differentiation or defaulting to head-to-head efficacy claims.

GSK/ViiV Healthcare — Cabenuva ($450M, +20%) + Dovato ($450M) + Apretude (~$130M)

GSK Q1 2026 call (April 2026):

  1. Cabenuva at ~$450M, +20% — the long-acting treatment category is maturing: ViiV reported that Cabenuva growth is moderating (from +30% in Q3 to +20% in Q1), reflecting market saturation among early-adopter patients and clinics. However, ViiV emphasized that penetration is still only ~5% of treated HIV patients, implying significant room for growth as more clinics build injection infrastructure.

  2. ViiV acknowledged Sunlenca competitive threat but emphasized Cabenuva's infrastructure moat: Management stated that Cabenuva's every-2-months regimen has established clinic workflows, payer coverage, and patient support programs that lenacapavir (twice-yearly) must replicate. The "infrastructure moat" argument is relevant — even a superior product faces adoption friction without the operational backend.

  3. Dovato flat at ~$450M — ViiV effectively ceding the oral 2-drug category: ViiV's investment is visibly shifting to Cabenuva and next-generation long-acting (cabotegravir LA + lenacapavir partnership discussions). Dovato is not being actively promoted against IDVYNSO, suggesting ViiV sees the competitive threat from Merck as manageable within the oral segment.

  4. ViiV's overall HIV portfolio (~$1.8B quarterly) reflects seasonal softness but structural stability: The $7B+ annual HIV franchise is the second-largest after Gilead's, and ViiV's dual-format strategy (oral Dovato + injectable Cabenuva) gives them coverage across patient preference segments.

Implication for Merck: ViiV's de-emphasis of Dovato creates a tactical opening for IDVYNSO. If the oral 2-drug "opponent" is reducing promotional intensity, IDVYNSO can potentially win the oral 2-drug conversation by default — provided Merck's reps actively position against Dovato (not just Biktarvy). The SFE study should track IDVYNSO vs. Dovato messaging as a separate competitive dynamic from IDVYNSO vs. Biktarvy.

Synthesis: What the Combined Q1 2026 HIV Picture Tells Us

  1. IDVYNSO launches into a $28B+ combined competitor franchise (Gilead $20B+ / ViiV $7B+). The absolute scale of the incumbents is the defining challenge. Merck's entire HIV revenue is <$500M. This is a David vs. two Goliaths situation — IDVYNSO wins by finding underserved niches, not by challenging incumbents head-on.

  2. Lenacapavir's trajectory ($50M → $80M → $120M in three quarters) signals that long-acting is accelerating faster than expected. If Sunlenca receives PrEP approval and PURPOSE 2 confirms PURPOSE 1's efficacy, the entire HIV market narrative shifts from "which daily pill" to "how often do I take something." IDVYNSO's oral daily positioning must anticipate this shift.

  3. ViiV's Dovato de-prioritization is the most actionable competitive opening. Dovato is the direct 2-drug oral comparator to IDVYNSO. ViiV investing in Cabenuva over Dovato means reduced Dovato promotional intensity — Merck can win the "oral 2-drug" category if reps are trained to position IDVYNSO as the superior oral simplification option (non-INSTI, TDF-free, NRTTI novelty).

  4. The strategic question for Merck's $15B HIV ambition is whether the oral daily market is growing or contracting. Biktarvy (+6%) is still growing, suggesting the oral market is not yet contracting. But Cabenuva (+20%) and Sunlenca (exponential ramp) are growing faster — indicating the long-acting segment is taking a growing share of market expansion. IDVYNSO's long-term value depends on whether oral daily remains the majority of HIV prescribing through 2030, or whether long-acting captures the growth.

4. Commercial Pulse

Metric IDVYNSO (Projected) Dovato Biktarvy
FDA Status Approved Apr 21, 2026 Approved Approved
Availability May 11, 2026 Available Available
Estimated 2033 Revenue (7MM) ~$1.8B Declining (patent expiry 2028-2030) $13B+ (declining post-patent)
SOV (Pre-Launch) 28% (as Merck overall) 30% (ViiV) 42% (Gilead)
Key Differentiation Non-INSTI, TDF-free, 2-drug, H2H vs BIK 2-drug INSTI, weight advantage Market leader, 5yr data, rapid initiation

What's changed commercially:

  • IDVYNSO promotion begins May 1, 2026
  • Merck reps exclusively promote IDVYNSO (Pifeltro promotion stops)
  • Sampling strategy expected to follow the ZoomRx sampling study recommendation: sales rep delivery as primary channel
  • Patient support program launched: co-pay assistance for commercially insured patients, Merck Access Program for others

What's missing:

  • No IDVYNSO commercial data yet (pre-launch)
  • No competitor counter-detailing data
  • No post-launch prescription tracking
  • No treatment-naive label expansion timeline

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Key Evidence This Quarter
1. Niche vs platform framing CRITICAL — RESOLVING NOW IDVYNSO approval + treatment-naive CROI data give Merck the ammunition for broad platform positioning. But the current label is switch-only. The field force's messaging choice in May-July will set the trajectory. Wave 1 SFE data is the first test.
2. Gilead SOV dominance UNCHANGED No structural change in sales force dynamics. Merck's SOV was 28% as of Q4 2025. IDVYNSO launch may create temporary uplift, but sustained change requires investment.
3. Oral > injectable STRENGTHENED IDVYNSO's approval validates the oral simplification thesis. A new, clinically differentiated oral option entering the market signals that oral innovation is alive. Meanwhile, lenacapavir for broad treatment is still 2-3 years from approval.
4. Sampling as launch lever RESOLVING Launch is imminent. Whether Merck executes rep-first sampling strategy (per ZoomRx recommendation) will be visible within weeks.
5. Islatravir perception risk LIVE — FIRST TEST FDA approval is the strongest safety validation. But physician adoption speed will reveal residual perception concerns. If IDs adopt slower than PCPs (counterintuitive for a new HIV drug), the islatravir history may be the explanation.

Net assessment: This is the most significant quarter in Merck's HIV franchise in a decade. IDVYNSO is approved. The data is strong. The sales force is improving. The measurement architecture is in place. The only remaining variable is commercial execution — and ZoomRx is uniquely positioned to measure it.

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q2 2026)

  1. IDVYNSO launch week (May 1-11) — field force deployment, sampling rollout, initial HCP reactions. Any early signals from Merck's internal tracking.
  2. ZoomRx DOR/ISL SFE Wave 1 fielding begins — target: 4-6 weeks post-launch. First interactions captured by mid-June, topline by late July.
  3. Competitor response — Gilead and ViiV counter-detailing strategies. Watch for: new Biktarvy messaging, increased Dovato promotion intensity, Cabenuva push as injectable alternative.
  4. Merck Q2 2026 earnings (July) — first quarter with partial IDVYNSO revenue. Even 2-3 weeks of launch data will signal initial trajectory. Management commentary on launch performance.
  5. DOR/ISL treatment-naive sNDA filing — if Merck files the supplemental NDA for the treatment-naive indication, it signals confidence in broad positioning and expands the commercial opportunity.
  6. ISLEND trial updates — islatravir + lenacapavir once-weekly oral; Phase 3 enrollment/interim data. This is Merck's next-gen HIV bet.

7. Conversation Starters for Merck

  1. "Congratulations on IDVYNSO. You now have the clinical data to compete with Biktarvy. The question shifts entirely to execution: can your 28% SOV field force convert head-to-head data into prescriptions against Gilead's 42%? Our tracking gives you the answer by August." — Frames ZoomRx as the essential measurement partner for launch.

  2. "The CROI treatment-naive data changes the game. Your current label is switch-only, but you have Phase 3 non-inferiority in new patients. When you file the sNDA, that's a new tracking study, a new message set, a new competitive conversation. Let's start planning the measurement architecture now." — BD conversation for treatment-naive SFE expansion.

  3. "Your reps have been trained on Pifeltro's niche story for 7 years. IDVYNSO requires a fundamentally different lead — 'proven vs Biktarvy' not 'lipid profile and renal.' Our Wave 1 data will tell you within 8 weeks of launch whether the messaging pivot happened." — Positions ZoomRx's value as the message-framing diagnostic.

  4. "One thing to watch: islatravir's clinical history may create a quiet headwind with ID specialists. Our SFE design should capture this — a specific question on physician confidence in islatravir's safety profile. Want to add it?" — Adds value to study design while surfacing a real commercial risk.

  5. "We ran the only external study on HIV sampling preferences. Your target HCPs prefer rep delivery by 17 points. Biktarvy has 90% sampling penetration. What's your IDVYNSO sampling penetration target for Week 12?" — Creates urgency with a specific benchmark.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

CROI 2026 late-breaking data from three Phase 3 trials — DOR/ISL demonstrated non-inferior efficacy to Biktarvy at Week 48 in treatment-naive adults AND maintained virologic suppression at Week 96 in switch patients. The treatment-naive data is the game-changer — it opens the door to a label expansion that would make IDVYNSO competitive for ALL HIV patients, not just switch.
Merck will promote ONLY IDVYNSO — confirmed in 2026 SFE planning document. No more Pifeltro promotions. Full sales force pivot to a single product. This simplifies the rep's job but raises the stakes — every call must land the IDVYNSO story.
ZoomRx DOR/ISL SFE study locked: 2 waves in 2H 2026, N=90/wave, tracking DOR/ISL vs Dovato vs Biktarvy. First topline by late July, 4-6 weeks post-launch. This is the first external measurement of IDVYNSO commercial execution.

First non-INSTI complete 2-drug regimen — for INSTI-averse/intolerant patients

First-in-class NRTTI — islatravir represents a novel mechanism

What it means: The treatment-naive data changes the strategic calculus. 4-6 week buffer post-launch before fielding begins
HCPs from Merck's ~6K target list

ViiV investing in Cabenuva over Dovato means reduced Dovato promotional intensity — Merck can win the "oral 2-drug" category if reps are trained to position IDVYNSO as the superior oral simplification option (non-INSTI, TDF-free, NRTTI novelty).


The strategic question for Merck's $15B HIV ambition is whether the oral daily market is growing or contracting. Biktarvy (+6%) is still growing, suggesting the oral market is not yet contracting. But Cabenuva (+20%) and Sunlenca (exponential ramp) are growing faster — indicating the long-acting segment is taking a growing share of market expansion. IDVYNSO's long-term value depends on whether oral daily remains the majority of HIV prescribing through 2030, or whether long-acting captures the growth.

5. Thesis Update



Thesis
Status
Key Evidence This Quarter




1. Niche vs platform framing
CRITICAL — RESOLVING NOW
IDVYNSO approval + treatment-naive CROI data give Merck the ammunition for broad platform positioning. But the current label is switch-only. The field force's messaging choice in May-July will set the trajectory. Wave 1 SFE data is the first test.


2. Gilead SOV dominance
UNCHANGED
No structural change in sales force dynamics. Merck's SOV was 28% as of Q4 2025. IDVYNSO launch may create temporary uplift, but sustained change requires investment.


3. Oral > injectable
STRENGTHENED
IDVYNSO's approval validates the oral simplification thesis. A new, clinically differentiated oral option entering the market signals that oral innovation is alive. Meanwhile, lenacapavir for broad treatment is still 2-3 years from approval.


4. Sampling as launch lever
RESOLVING
Launch is imminent. Whether Merck executes rep-first sampling strategy (per ZoomRx recommendation) will be visible within weeks.


5. Islatravir perception risk
LIVE — FIRST TEST
FDA approval is the strongest safety validation. But physician adoption speed will reveal residual perception concerns. If IDs adopt slower than PCPs (counterintuitive for a new HIV drug), the islatravir history may be the explanation.



Net assessment: This is the most significant quarter in Merck's HIV franchise in a decade. IDVYNSO is approved. The data is strong. The sales force is improving. The measurement architecture is in place. The only remaining variable is commercial execution — and ZoomRx is uniquely positioned to measure it.

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q2 2026)

IDVYNSO launch week (May 1-11) — field force deployment, sampling rollout, initial HCP reactions. Any early signals from Merck's internal tracking.
ZoomRx DOR/ISL SFE Wave 1 fielding begins — target: 4-6 weeks post-launch. First interactions captured by mid-June, topline by late July.
Competitor response — Gilead and ViiV counter-detailing strategies. Watch for: new Biktarvy messaging, increased Dovato promotion intensity, Cabenuva push as injectable alternative.
Merck Q2 2026 earnings (July) — first quarter with partial IDVYNSO revenue. Even 2-3 weeks of launch data will signal initial trajectory. Management commentary on launch performance.
DOR/ISL treatment-naive sNDA filing — if Merck files the supplemental NDA for the treatment-naive indication, it signals confidence in broad positioning and expands the commercial opportunity.
ISLEND trial updates — islatravir + lenacapavir once-weekly oral; Phase 3 enrollment/interim data. This is Merck's next-gen HIV bet.
